name: laravel-security-audit
description: Security auditor for Laravel applications. Analyzes code for vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and insecure practices using OWASP standards and Laravel security best practices.
capabilities: Analyze Laravel controllers for authorization vulnerabilities and IDOR risks
Review input validation using FormRequests and validation rules
Audit authentication flows including Sanctum and JWT configuration
Check database queries for SQL injection and mass assignment issues
Evaluate file upload handling for MIME type and extension validation
Review API security including rate limiting and response sanitization
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static analysis findings are false positives. This is an educational/documentation skill containing prompt instructions for security auditing, not executable code. The detected patterns (external_commands, network, env_access) are references to security concepts being taught, not actual vulnerable code. No security risks identified.
valueStatement: Laravel developers need to ensure their applications follow security best practices. This skill provides comprehensive security auditing using OWASP standards and Laravel-specific security knowledge.
userTitle: Audit Laravel Apps for Security Vulnerabilities
limitations: Cannot execute code or run actual security scans on live applications
Analysis is limited to the code files provided for review
Cannot verify runtime configuration or environment settings
Does not replace professional penetration testing or security audits

useCases.targetUser: Laravel developers preparing for production deployment
useCases.title: Pre-Deployment Security Review
useCases.description: Review Laravel application code before deploying to production to identify security vulnerabilities.

useCases.targetUser: Development teams implementing secure coding practices
useCases.title: Code Review Assistance
useCases.description: Integrate security analysis into pull request reviews to catch vulnerabilities early.

useCases.title: Legacy Code Audit
useCases.description: Assess existing Laravel applications for security debt and misconfigurations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Security Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this Laravel controller for security vulnerabilities. Check for proper authorization, input validation, and common OWASP issues.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick security review of a single controller file

promptTemplates.title: Full Application Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a comprehensive security audit of this Laravel application. Analyze authentication, authorization, input validation, database queries, file uploads, and API security. Classify each finding by risk level.
promptTemplates.scenario: Complete security assessment before production deployment

promptTemplates.title: Authentication Flow Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Audit the authentication implementation in this Laravel application. Check password hashing, session management, token handling, and Sanctum/JWT configuration for security issues.
promptTemplates.scenario: Focused review of login and session management code

promptTemplates.title: API Security Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Evaluate the API endpoints in this Laravel application for security vulnerabilities. Check rate limiting, authorization, input validation, response sanitization, and mass assignment protection.
promptTemplates.scenario: Security review of REST API implementation

bestPractices: Always use FormRequest classes for input validation and authorization
Apply the principle of least privilege for database and file system access
Enable rate limiting on all public API endpoints to prevent abuse
antiPatterns: Using request()->all() without validation in create or update operations
Checking authorization only in controllers without policy enforcement
Storing uploaded files in publicly accessible directories without validation

faq.question: What Laravel versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for Laravel 10 and 11+ applications but can audit older versions with knowledge of version-specific security features.

faq.question: Can this skill scan my entire codebase automatically?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes code files you provide. For large codebases, provide key files like controllers, models, and middleware for focused security review.

faq.question: Does this replace penetration testing?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides static code analysis and security guidance. It complements but does not replace professional penetration testing or dynamic security scans.

faq.question: What OWASP categories does this cover?
faq.answer: Coverage includes Injection, Broken Authentication, Sensitive Data Exposure, XML External Entities, Broken Access Control, Security Misconfiguration, XSS, Insecure Deserialization, and Known Vulnerabilities.

faq.question: Can this skill fix vulnerabilities automatically?
faq.answer: The skill identifies vulnerabilities and provides secure code examples. You must manually review and implement the recommended fixes in your codebase.

faq.question: Is my code shared or stored when using this skill?
faq.answer: Code analysis happens within your session. However, avoid sharing sensitive credentials, API keys, or production secrets in any code snippets.
